name: iFlytek Spark V1.0 (Xinghuo)
note: iFlytek's first Spark cognitive model, one of China's earliest ChatGPT challengers; launched with speech I/O, image understanding added later in 2023.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed real launch event: multiple independent sources (Baidu Baike, chinaz.com announcement of the May 6 2023 release conference, and general web search corroboration) confirm iFlytek held its 'Xinghuo (Spark) Cognitive Large Model' achievement release conference on 2023-05-06, matching the record. The originally-cited news.cri.cn source could not be re-confirmed (it discusses a later SuperCLUE ranking, not the initial release), so it was replaced with a source that directly corroborates the release date; params/architecture remain correctly undisclosed (closed proprietary model), and no benchmark result rows were included, consistent with none being verifiable for this early proprietary release. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Widened, methodology-diversified search performed for iFlytek Spark V1.0 (Xinghuo), corpus/models/iflytek/iflytek-spark-v1-0-xinghuo.json. Read existing verificationNotes first (prior round already confirmed release date via chinaz.com/Baidu Baike; news.cri.cn was rejected only as a release-date source, not yet checked as a benchmark source).

Searched: (1) Chinese vendor/press — jiemian.com, techweb.com.cn, sina.com.cn finance, cnstock.com, news.cri.cn, zhidx.com/智东西, qbitai.com/量子位, jiqizhixin.com/机器之心; (2) Chinese leaderboards — SuperCLUE/cluebenchmarks.com, OpenCompass/司南 (incl. CompassArena); (3) international — LMSYS/Chatbot Arena leaderboard blog archives, Artificial Analysis, OpenRouter; (4) Wayback Machine snapshots of xinghuo.xfyun.cn (tool unavailable in this environment — web.archive.org fetch blocked).

Real, citable result found: SuperCLUE benchmark, published 2023-05-09 (3 days after the 2023-05-06 V1.0 launch, and a month before the 2023-06-09 V1.5 launch, so almost certainly testing V1.0). iFlytek Spark scored 53.58 overall (basic ability 74, Chinese-characteristics 44, academic/professional 42.73), ranked 3rd of 9 models globally / 1st domestically, trailing GPT-4 by 23pts. Independently corroborated by 5 Chinese press outlets (jiemian.com, techweb.com.cn, sina.com.cn, cnstock.com, news.cri.cn). NOT added because "superclue" is not in the allowed taxonomy slug list, and it is not equivalent to c-eval/cmmlu/mmlu (different methodology/questions) — forcing it into one of those slugs would misrepresent the source data, which the task explicitly prohibits.

Also found but rejected: iFlytek's own launch-day claims (zhidx.com) of a proprietary "481-subtask/7-dimension" internal AGI eval framework with a vague English-comprehension score (ChatGPT 4.48 vs Spark 4.29 on an undefined 5-point scale) — self-reported, non-standardized, no taxonomy mapping. All discoverable C-Eval/CMMLU/MMLU numeric scores for "Spark" belong to later versions (V2.0/V3.0/V4.0), not V1.0 — V1.0 was closed/no public API and appears to have never been run through standard international or Chinese academic benchmark suites before being superseded a month later by V1.5.

Verdict: no qualifying addition exists. This is a genuine no-data outcome for a short-lived (~1 month), API-less, pre-standard-benchmark-era proprietary release — recommend closing out further search rounds for this specific model unless a future methodology explicitly adds a SuperCLUE-equivalent taxonomy slug. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): Checked all four sources specified in the task for iFlytek Spark V1.0 (Xinghuo), plus prior verificationNotes in corpus/models/iflytek/iflytek-spark-v1-0-xinghuo.json confirm 6 prior rounds already exhausted general web/Chinese/Japanese/regional-leaderboard search. This round adds no data:

1. Epoch AI benchmarks.json (https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json, 179KB) — contains only benchmark *metadata* (definitions of gpqa-diamond, frontiermath, swe-bench-verified, etc.), no per-model scores.

2. Epoch AI benchmarks.csv (https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv, 2.29MB, 5981 rows) — this is the actual model x benchmark results feed. Grepped case-insensitively for "iflytek", "xinghuo", "讯飞", "星火", and "spark" across all columns (model id, display name, org, etc.). Zero matches for iFlytek/Xinghuo in any form. The only "spark" hits are for Meta AI's unrelated "Muse Spark" / "muse-spark" model (a... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] No qualifying addition found. iFlytek Spark V1.0 (Xinghuo) genuinely has neither an indexable HuggingFace repo nor a paper-published benchmark table attributable to V1.0 specifically. Detail on both required source types:

1) HUGGINGFACE MODEL CARD CHECK — no repo exists. The official org page https://huggingface.co/iFLYTEK explicitly shows "None public yet" for both models (0) and datasets (0) — iFlytek has never published Spark weights or a model card to HF (consistent with the model being fully closed/proprietary, apiAvailable:false in the corpus). Broad searches ("iFlytek Spark huggingface.co model card", site:huggingface.co, and Chinese-language "讯飞星火 huggingface.co") surfaced only unrelated repos: licyk/Spark-Chemistry-X1-13B (a 2025-era community fine-tune of the much-later "Spark-X1" reasoning model, unrelated to 2023 V1.0), several "iflytek" *datasets* (XiangPan/iflytek, fenffef/iflytek, mteb/IFlyTek) which are copies of the unrelated IFLYTEK NLP classification benchmark dataset (a 119-class app-description text-classification corpus, nothing to do with the Spark LLM), and hfl (Joint Laboratory of HIT and iFLYTEK Research) which hosts BERT-era encoder models, not Spark. No "Files and versions" eval_results.json exists anywhere because no Spark repo exists. Verdict: source type 1 is a genuine dead end for this model.

2) SEMANTIC SCHOLAR / CITATION SEARCH — API itself was persistently rate-limited (HTTP 429 on 4 separate attempts against api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search with several query variants), so I fell back to WebSearch-based citation discovery, which did surface two real, fetched, citable papers that reference "Spark" by name, both read directly (via arXiv PDF page extraction, not just search snippets):

  a) "Generative Input: Towards Next-Generation Input Methods Paradigm" (Ding et al., iFLYTEK Research / USTC, arXiv:2311.01166, also ACL 2024 Findings) — Section 4.1.4 "Base Models" states verbatim: "all the experiments in this paper are based on the 2.6B version of iFlytek's self-developed LLM - Spark⁴... The model has a GPT-like structure containing 32 layers of transformers and is equipped with strong text generation capability after pre-training with a large amount of Chinese corpus," with footnote 4 linking to https://xinghuo.xfyun.cn/ (the same product as this corpus entry). REJECTED as a source for two independent reasons: (i) version ambiguity — the paper (Nov 2023) never says "V1.0"; given iFlytek's rapid release cadence that year (V1.0 May, V1.5 June, V2.0 Aug, V3.0 Oct 2023) and that this is a distinct low-latency "2.6B" distilled variant for an IME product (not the flagship chatbot), it cannot be responsibly attributed to V1.0 specifically; (ii) even if attribution were solid, the only scores reported are task-specific P@1/P@5 and human MOS ratings on iFlytek's own proprietary IntelAssoc/ConvAssist/FK2C input-method tasks (Tables 2-5) — none of which map to any allowed taxonomy slug.

  b) "C-Eval: A Multi-Level Multi-Discipline Chinese Evaluation Suite for Foundation Models" (arXiv:2305.08322, May 2023 — same month as V1.0's launch) — read via ar5iv HTML rendering. This paper explicitly EXCLUDES Xinghuo from scoring, stating verbatim: "there are some other commercial Chinese-oriented LLMs whose weights and APIs are not directly open to the public at the time of writing this paper, such as Wenxin Yiyan (Baidu), Tongyi Qianwen (Alibaba), and Xunfei Xinghuo (iFLYTEK)." No score/table row exists for it. This is a negative-but-citable confirmation, consistent with the corpus's own apiAvailable:false field and prior rounds' conclusion that V1.0 predates the model's entry into standardized benchmark suites.

  Also checked and found irrelevant/inaccessible: arXiv:2402.01723 and the Springer "physics concept inventories" Spark study (paywalled, and near-certainly testing a 2024-era Spark version, not V1.0 from May 2023).

Combined with the 19 prior rounds already logged in verificationNotes (general web, Chinese/Japanese press, SuperCLUE, Epoch AI CSV, Vals.ai, OpenCompass, LMSYS, Wayback snapshots — all exhausted with no taxonomy-mappable result), this round's two specifically-requested source types (HF model card, Semantic Scholar/citation search) also come up empty for V1.0. This is a well-corroborated genuine no-data case: a ~1-month-lived, API-less, pre-standard-benchmark-era proprietary release that was superseded before ever being run through a benchmark suite this taxonomy recognizes.

[ROUND 21 — LIGHT DELTA-CHECK, 2026-07-16] Per task instructions, performed only a light re-check (not a full re-derivation) since the most recent logged round (ROUND 20) is dated the same day, 2026-07-16, leaving effectively no time gap in which new information could plausibly have surfaced. Two targeted web searches run: (1) "iFlytek Spark V1.0 Xinghuo 讯飞星火 V1.0 benchmark score 2026", (2) "\"Xinghuo\" OR \"iFlytek Spark\" V1.0 MMLU GPQA benchmark leaderboard". Both returned only material on later Xinghuo generations (X1, X1.5, X2-Flash, V4.0 — e.g. Spark X1.5's Gaokao-math and reasoning-benchmark claims) and general MMLU/GPQA leaderboard sites (llm-stats.com, artificialanalysis.ai, pricepertoken.com) with no V1.0-specific entries. No new citable V1.0 result found. Reaffirms the 20 prior rounds' verdict: genuine no-data outcome for this short-lived, API-less, pre-standard-benchmark-era 2023-05 release. No further action needed; recommend no additional rounds unless a new source type becomes available.
